Is there a solid waste dump site OR wastewater pond in vicinity of TW?
Is the source water hardness within permissible limit? [below 500 mg/L as CaCO3]
Is the borehole properly covered?
Is the water quality of the source monitored (e.g. is there any regular periodic sampling & testing from the source?
Have any water shortages been experienced because of reduced yield of the source?
answer_options: No shortages experienced|Yes, shortages experienced for less than 3 months duration|Yes, shortages experienced for more than 3 months duration

question_text: Is another public TW available within a distance of 100 m (330 ft)?
Are there any leakages from either the Rising main OR the Supply main?
Does the Rising main or the Supply main traverse through a wastewater drain/ditch or in such proximity that wastewater can reach the pipe?
Is the water in the storage reservoir exposed to contamination (e.g. broken or missing access cover, broken ventilator openings)?
Are there any leakages in the distribution network?
Do the pipes in the distribution network traverse through a wastewater drain/ditch or in such proximity that wastewater can reach the pipes?
Are illegal connections from the network common?
What is the distribution network layout?
Is the water supply chlorinated?
Is residual chlorine value in the distribution network monitored?
Is the water quality in the distribution network monitored (e.g. is there any regular periodic sampling & testing from the distribution network?
Are there any public standposts in the network?
Have the households in the service area their own connections at their premises?
Are there households who do not have their own connections but collect water from nearby connections or public standposts?
Does the scheme include water storage reservoir with a capacity that can meet the community's water needs for a period of?
answer_options: One day|Half day|Quarter day|No storage tank

question_text: What is the approximate pressure head at the critical points (farthest in the network OR at high elevations)?
answer_options: More than 20 ft|Between 10-20ft|Between 5-10 ft|Less than 5 ft

question_text: Is the water supply to the consumers Continuous (24 hrs a day)?
Is the scheme Gravity based?
If the scheme is Pumping based then what is the source of electricity?
On average (roughly) how often the scheme requires major repairs?
answer_options: Once every year|Once every 6 months|Once every 3 months|Once every month

question_text: If the scheme is located in a flood-prone area then has the scheme been designed for protection against floods?
If the scheme is located in a drought-prone area then has the scheme been designed for resilience against droughts?
If the scheme is vulnerable to GLOFs then has the scheme been designed for protection against GLOFs?
Officials in charge of the scheme who have completed a course in WASH in Emergencies? (Leave empty if no one has completed the course)
Who operates the scheme?
How soon are most complaints resolved?
Does a climate resilient water safety plan exist for the scheme that is regularly updated?
Does the department itself, OR in coordination with the District Administration, carry out periodic (every 5 years or less) disaster preparedness drills/ simulations/ exercises?
Does the department have access to updated and location specific (at least district level) climate change projections?
Has an Emergency Response Plan (ERP) been prepared at the Division (XEN) level OR does the District Level ERP contains water supply component?
Does any mechanism exist to coordinate with the district administration, health & education departments?
Is the broader area within which the scheme is located, vulnerable to floods?
Is the broader area within which the scheme is located, vulnerable to droughts?
Is the broader area within which the scheme is located, vulnerable to GLOFs?
Are other safe water sources are available in the area which can supply the basic needs of the consumers in case of emergencies or long breakdowns?

Does the watershed include activities / land uses that can introduce serious contaminants into the water source? [Examples include urban areas within the watershed, industrial activities, mining activities, agricultural lands using chemical fertilizers & pesticides]
Are there any interventions for recharge of groundwater such as MAR, check dams, infiltration galleries OR water conservation measures such as metering, rainwater harvesting, reuse of wastewater?
Does that watershed contain ecosystem/ nature based interventions for floods mitigation?
Is the community involved in decision making e.g. in planning, operations of the WSS?
Is the community involved/consulted/informed about emergency response plans?
Is access available to the internet through either mobile data or cable/wired services?
Are there any reasons to believe that the literacy is BETTER or WORSE than the rest of the district? Please provide any such reasons:
What would be the average time (return journey) for the majority of the community to fetch water if the scheme becomes non-functional due to any reason? [Time in minutes]
Please provide any reasons that suggest that this area is either SAFER or LESS SAFE for women & children than the rest of the district:
Compared to the overall situation in the district, does this area have BETTER or WORSE access to emergency services ( medical and relief)? Please provide reasons for either of the case:
Does the coverage area of the scheme include any disadvantaged or vulnerable groups due to ethnic, religious, poverty reasons? Please provide comments:
